old_value: {"id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.48.3","title":"Fix RFC 8613 nonce and option encoding","description":"Rust OSCORE nonce construction XORs Sender ID length with PIV length instead of encoding Sender ID length, no-PIV responses use responder identity instead of the request's complete nonce, empty response OSCORE options encode as 0x00 instead of empty, and parser accepts reserved bits/trailing bytes. Correct these RFC 8613 interoperability defects with independent published-vector or literal formula tests.","acceptance_criteria":"Nonce formula matches RFC 8613 Section 5.2 literals; no-PIV response reuses request nonce identity; empty option is zero bytes; reserved bits 5-7 and trailing bytes with k=0 reject; existing RFC vectors and roundtrips pass; no_std/release/all-feature tests, Clippy, and three reviews pass.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"Mark Atwood","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-17T23:20:57.243022Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-17T23:38:41.82323Z","started_at":"2026-07-17T23:21:10.944285Z","closed_at":"2026-07-17T23:38:41.82323Z","close_reason":"Implemented and validated with RFC-literal tests; debug/release/all-feature/no-default suites and library Clippy pass; three hash-bound reviews found no remaining in-scope P0-P2 defects. Context lifecycle remains .48.2.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.48.3","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.48","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-17T23:20:57.459214Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
new_value: {"notes":"2026-07-23 empirical confirmation (cross-impl review): (1) lib.rs:1031 nonce[0]=sender_id.len()^piv.len() must be sender_id.len() - unpatched Rust fails RFC C.4 (got ee062416a517afb99ce4b70b4f, expected 612f1092f1776f1c1668b3825e); with a 1-byte patch Rust reproduces C.4, C.5, C.8 ciphertexts EXACTLY, proving key derivation, AAD, and option encoding are otherwise RFC-correct. (2) unprotect_response nonce uses recipient_id (lib.rs:591-595) even when the response omits PIV; RFC requires the requester's ID_PIV (request nonce) - patched-Rust unprotect of RFC C.7 response ciphertext dbaad1e9... fails DecryptFailed; C.8 OK. (3) protect_response(include_piv=false) likewise uses sender_id (lib.rs:506-510); produces 789a9bc3... vs RFC C.7 dbaad1e9... - DIFF even after the byte-0 patch. (4) Empty response OSCORE option encoded as one 0x00 byte (lib.rs:548) vs RFC/aiocoap empty value b'' - NOTE the C implementation shares this (oscore.c:1861-1862), both non-canonical but mutually consistent; parsers accept both. (5) C picked the CORRECT ID for no-PIV responses (recipient_id = requester ID, oscore.c:1806) - C and Rust picked opposite IDs here, so C-vs-Rust response interop fails even where each is self-consistent."}
